pr.title: Non-record: Depth Recurrence in Parameter-Constrained Transformers — What Works, What Doesn't, and Why

output.summary: Depth-recurrent Middle-Cycle stack vs. flat baseline, showing Noisy QAT keeps quantization error from compounding across shared looped blocks.
techniques: Middle-Cycle stem/core/tail layout with repeated shared blocks
Noisy QAT matching int8 noise to tame recurrence quantization
Controlled flat vs. looped A/B to isolate depth recurrence costs
